case_title: PARADISE ON 25TH CONDOMINIUMS ASSOCIATION VS. BRIAN L. MARTINEZ, AN UNMARRIED MAN

APPLICATION FOR ENTRY OF SISTER STATE JUDGMENT; NOTICE OF SISTER STATE JUDGMENT ISSUED FILED BY PLAINTIFF PARADISE ON 25TH CONDOMINIUMS ASSOCIATION AS TO DEFENDANT BRIAN L. MARTINEZ, AN UNMARRIED MAN JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED
NOTICE OF CHAPTER 13 BANKRUPTCY CASE AND OF AUTOMATIC STAY FILED BY DEFENDANT BRIAN L. MARTINEZ, AN UNMARRIED MAN
OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S MOTION TO VACATE CIVIL ASSESSMENT JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF PARADISE ON 25TH CONDOMINIUMS ASSOCIATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRIAN L. MARTINEZ, MOTION TO VACATE CIVIL ASSESMENT JUDGMENT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - CONTINUED TO MAY 4, 2011. MOVING PARTY TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. SEE SF LOCAL RULE 2.6B.
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAY-04-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

UNDER APPLICATION TO THE CLERK FOR JUDGMENT UNDER SEC 1710.25 C.C.P., IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF PARADISE ON 25TH CONDOMINIUMS ASSOCIATION RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT BRIAN L. MARTINEZ, AN UNMARRIED MAN $15,538.00 PRINCIPAL, $258.97 INTEREST, $370.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $16,166.97, SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT ALL FURTHER PROCESSING TO BE DONE BY DEFAULT SECTION
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-11-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRIAN L. MARTINEZ MOTION TO VACATE CIVIL ASSESMENT JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE FEES AND ASSESSMENTS ACCRUED AFTER DEFENDANT FILED FOR BANKRUPTCY. ACCORDINGLY, THE ACTIONS REGARDING THESE FEES AND ASSESSMENTS WERE NOT SUBJECT TO THE STAY UNDER THE BANKRUPTCY CODE. SEE 11 U.S.C.A. SECTION 362(A)(1). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT BRIAN L. MARTINEZ MOTION TO VACATE CIVIL ASSESMENT JUDGMENT.

APPEARANCES: Randal J. Ivor-Smith, Esq. of Raines Feldman LLP for pltf & opposing party (appearing via CourtCall); Brian L. Martinez, 676-8582 deft & moving party in propria persona.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO VACATE CIVIL ASSESSMENT IS DENIED. THE FEES AND ASSESSMENTS ACCRUED AFTER DEFENDANT FILED FOR BANKRUPTCY. ACCORDINGLY, THE ACTIONS REGARDING THESE FEES AND ASSESSMENTS WERE NOT SUBJECT TO THE STAY UNDER THE BANKRUPTCY CODE. SEE 11 U.S.C.A. SECTION 362(a)(1). Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
